channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 2 hours.

item.title: 🏛️ Pentagon leaders place $25 billon price tag on Operation Epic Fury | Breaking Defense
item.description: Members of the House Armed Services Committee also pressed Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth on the recent firing of Army and Navy leaders.

item.title: 🏛️ Marines to field light loitering munition to operational units in ‘June timeframe’: Official | Breaking Defense
item.description: Currently OFP-L systems from three manufacturers are being tested by units at Dugway Proving Grounds outside of Salt Lake City.

item.title: 🌏 SPACECOM’s FY27 spending boost wish list? Empty. | Breaking Defense
item.description: US Space Command’s plan to ask for no additional FY27 funds follows a similar move by US Indo-Pacific Command.

item.title: 📍 Trump’s War Is Damaging U.S. Arms Exports to Allies | Foreign Policy
item.description: Deals are being canceled as stockpiles run short.

item.title: 📍 Can South Africa’s Apartheid-Era Negotiator Chart a Smooth Course in the U.S.? | Foreign Policy
item.description: Pretoria stakes high hopes on its controversial pick for ambassador to Washington.

item.title: 🏛️ Marines Realize They Can’t Depend On Army For Ballistic Missile Defense | The War Zone
item.description: The Marines currently rely on already strained Army units to provide defense against incoming ballistic missiles, which could be a big problem in the Pacific. The post Marines Realize They Can’t Depend On Army For Ballistic Missile Defense appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 The Global Economic Impact from the Iran Conflict | Foreign Policy
item.description: Gita Gopinath on how countries can make themselves more resilient.

item.title: 📍 What swap, Gulf? | Atlantic Council
item.description: Amidst the chaos of the conflict in the Middle East, there’s been a buzz around offering dollar currency swaps to allies. Those have traditionally been the territory of central banks, but this time around, Treasury is taking the lead. The post What swap, Gulf? appeared first on…

item.title: 📍 Safran, MBDA see delivery of French rocket artillery in 2029 as feasible | Defense News
item.description: The companies said Thundart is fully designed and produced in France.

item.title: 🏛️ Brain function evaluations to be part of Marine health records | Defense News
item.description: By September, Marines will be screened for blast exposure and overpressure during their periodic health assessments, a Pentagon official said.

item.title: 🏛️ Marines Offer Glimpse Of New Plan For Its Future Ground Combat Forces | The War Zone
item.description: Ground Combat Element 2040 offers hints into how the Marines expect to fight in a future dominated by AI and autonomous weapons. The post Marines Offer Glimpse Of New Plan For Its Future Ground Combat Forces appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ First USMC MQ-58 Valkyrie CCA Drones To Arrive In 2029 | The War Zone
item.description: The MQ-58 with conventional landing gear will mark the beginning of the Marines’ operational Collaborative Combat Aircraft journey, with other types likely to follow. The post First USMC MQ-58 Valkyrie CCA Drones To Arrive In 2029 appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏴 Estonia’s CV90 exit has no ‘big effect’ on joint European order: BAE Hägglunds chief | Breaking Defense
item.description: “The effect is marginal. I think the other customers will get vehicles earlier,” Tommy Gustafsson-Rask, general manager of BAE Systems Hägglunds, said during a media visit to its Ornskoldsvik site.

item.title: 🏛️ Navy releases list of ships it plans to inactivate this year | Defense News
item.description: The service intends to remove 14 vessels from its fleet.

item.title: ⚛️ One big FAMMily: Air Force eyes huge boost for low-cost cruise missile | Breaking Defense
item.description: With nearly 30,000 copies envisioned, even among a spending spree for munitions, the Air Force’s Family of Affordable Mass Missile stands out.

item.title: 🌏 US Marines help gun down beach invaders in simulated Philippines defense | Defense News
item.description: Afterwards, as the repulsed “enemy” withdrew in disorder, a coup de grace was delivered by an explosive-laden, first-person-view drone.

item.title: 📍 Belgian defense minister meeting with firms lined up for slice of $1.3B counter-drone pie | Breaking Defense
item.description: A spokesperson for the Belgian Ministry of Defense confirmed to Breaking Defense that the tender will combine both active and passive measures, including detection sensors and a command and control system to link all effectors.

item.title: 🏴 When one of these hits the ocean’s surface at 100Gs, enemy submarines better hide | Breaking Defense
item.description: [Sponsored] Next-gen sonobuoys for anti-submarine warfare are specifically designed to detect quieter Russian and Chinese subs.

item.title: 🌍 Drone diplomacy wins Ukraine valuable allies, but now it must deliver | Defense News
item.description: The Iran war has confirmed how central drones are to modern warfare and handed Zelenskiy a diplomatic trump card, analysts say.

item.title: 📍 The United States-Cuba Oil Embargo and International Law | Just Security
item.description: Experts on naval warfare examine the international law dimensions of the United States campaign against Cuba. The post The United States-Cuba Oil Embargo and International Law appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🏛️ USS Wasp sees five-year service life extension | Breaking Defense
item.description: “The other LHDs — we’ve got to study to see if we can extend them, and the plan is going to be to do that,” a top Marine said.

item.title: 📍 The Governance Gap Mythos Exposed—And How to Address It | Just Security
item.description: When the consequences of one corporate decision can compromise the world’s digital infrastructure, industry self-governance is not enough. The post The Governance Gap Mythos Exposed—And How to Address It appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🏴 War Is Killing Ukraine’s Oligarchy | Foreign Policy
item.description: The fight against Russia is accomplishing what years of struggle against corruption couldn’t.

item.title: 🌍 Early Edition: April 29, 2026 | Just Security
item.description: Signup to receive the Early Edition in your inbox here. A curated weekday guide to major news and developments over the last 24 hours. Here’s today’s news: IRAN WAR – CEASEFIRE President Trump has instructed aides to prepare for an extended blockade of Iran as negotiations…

item.title: 🌍 Charai for The Jerusalem Strategic Tribune: The Crown’s Moral Voice: King Charles in Washington and the Test of Western Clarity | Atlantic Council
item.description: The post Charai for The Jerusalem Strategic Tribune: The Crown’s Moral Voice: King Charles in Washington and the Test of Western Clarity appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 Hedging Is the New Normal | Foreign Policy
item.description: For the foreseeable future, successful statecraft will depend on hedging.

item.title: 🏴 The EU Is the New Go-To Middle Power | Foreign Policy
item.description: In a world of disorder, the bloc’s boring stability is suddenly attractive.

item.title: 🌏 The Taiwan Evacuation Trap | Foreign Policy
item.description: Washington has no good options for evacuating Americans in a crisis.

item.title: 📍 German Foreign Policy Has Finally Grown Up | Foreign Policy
item.description: Friedrich Merz is, slowly but surely, putting his country on a new international footing.

item.title: 📍 Aligning the U.S. and Canadian Defense Industrial Bases | War on the Rocks
item.description: The United States and Canada are both racing to rebuild their defense industrial bases, recognizing that future conflicts will be determined not only by military capability, but by the ability to produce at scale. But they cannot succeed alone — and importantly, they do not need…
